text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道节目。

text: East Coast fever kills hundreds of thousands of cattle in eastern and central Africa every year.
zh: 每年,东海岸热病都会导致非洲东部和中部数十万头牛死亡。

text: The animals become infected when they get bitten by ticks carrying the parasite that causes the disease.
zh: 当牛被携带致病寄生虫的蜱虫叮咬后,就会被感染。

text: disease.
zh: 疾病。

text: A parasite is an organism that feeds on other organisms.
zh: 寄生虫是一种依靠其他生物体生存的有机体。

text: Donald Knowles directs the Animal Disease Research Unit in the Agricultural Research Service, part of the United States Department of Agriculture.
zh: 唐纳德·诺尔斯(Donald Knowles)是美国农业部农业研究局动物疾病研究室的主任。

text: The service is working with the International Livestock Research Institute in Kenya
zh: 该研究局正与肯尼亚国际家畜研究所合作,

text: to find better ways to prevent parasitic diseases in cattle.
zh: 寻找更好的方法来预防牛的寄生虫病。

text: Dr. Knowles is also a professor at Washington State University.
zh: 诺尔斯博士同时也是华盛顿州立大学的教授。

text: He points out that East Coast fever is not a contagious disease, so animals cannot give it to each other.
zh: 他指出,东海岸热病并不是一种接触传染病,牛与牛之间不会直接传染。

text: You need a tick to transmit from one infected cow to the other.
zh: 需要蜱虫作为媒介,才能把病原从一头感染的牛传给另一头。

text: They won't transmit just by standing next to each other.
zh: 仅仅站在一起,牛是不会互相传染的。

text: You need a tick to move it between them.
zh: 需要蜱虫在它们之间传播病原。

text: Farmers in parts of Africa use a vaccination method against East Coast fever known as infect and treat.
zh: 非洲部分地区的农民采用一种被称为“感染再治疗”的方法来预防东海岸热病。

text: First, they infect animals with live parasites.
zh: 首先,他们让动物感染活的寄生虫。

text: Then, they treat them with drugs to help them recover.
zh: 然后,用药物治疗,帮助它们恢复。

text: That way, explains Dr. Knowles, the animal becomes immunized or vaccinated against a full case of the deadly fever.
zh: 诺尔斯博士解释说,这样一来,动物就会对这种致命热病产生免疫,相当于接种了疫苗。

text: But there are problems with this method of vaccination.
zh: 但这种接种方法也存在一些问题。

text: vaccination.
zh: 接种方法。

text: Uninfected ticks that bite the vaccinated animal can still get infected and spread the parasite to other animals.
zh: 未被感染的蜱虫叮咬过接种动物后,仍可能被感染,并把寄生虫传播给其他动物。

text: Dr. Knowles says once a cow is infected with this parasite, all the data indicates that the cow stays infected for life.
zh: 诺尔斯博士说,一旦牛感染了这种寄生虫,所有数据都表明,这头牛将终生带有该病原。

text: That means that That cow, even a cow that has been vaccinated, now it's protected against the disease, but it can still transmit.
zh: 这意味着,那头牛——即便是接种过疫苗的牛——虽然已经对这种病有了免疫力,但仍然可能传播病原。

text: See, it's infected for life.
zh: 要知道,它是终身感染的。

text: And so that animal is still a source of transmission.
zh: 因此,这头牛仍然是传染源。

text: And there are other problems with this method of vaccination.
zh: 而这种接种方法还存在其他问题。

text: vaccination.
zh: 接种方法。

text: One of the things in using live vaccines is that they usually require cold storage,
zh: 使用活疫苗的问题之一是,它们通常需要低温保存,

text: and that adds to the cost of it and also adds to the difficulty of using it in places like Africa.
zh: 这不仅增加了成本,也增加了在非洲这类地区使用的难度。

text: For more than four years, American and Kenyan scientists have studied diseases carried by ticks.
zh: 四年多来,美国和肯尼亚的科学家一直在研究由蜱虫传播的疾病。

text: The scientists in the United States centered their work on the parasite that causes Texas cattle fever.
zh: 美国的科学家把研究重点放在了导致德克萨斯牛热病的寄生虫上。

text: This fever is common to the Americas.
zh: 这种热病在美洲很常见。

text: This year, the scientists began a new study in Africa.
zh: 今年,科学家们在非洲展开了一项新的研究。

text: The study is aimed at developing a new vaccine for the East Coast fever parasite.
zh: 这项研究旨在开发一种针对东海岸热病寄生虫的新疫苗。

text: Dr. Knowles says their goal is a vaccine that does not require infecting and treating animals or the need for cold storage.
zh: 诺尔斯博士说,他们的目标是研制出一种不需要“感染再治疗”、也不需要低温保存的疫苗。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villareal.
zh: 我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔(Alex Villareal),这里是美国之音特别英语节目。
